title: Alexitoxicon darvasicum
taxonomy: Alexitoxicon darvasicum is classified as a genus of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This classification makes it a part of the borage family, which includes plants like borage and heliotrope. The genus Alexitoxicon contains several species, with Alexitoxicon darvasicum being one of them. The plant is native to Central Asia and is known for its toxic properties. Within the Boraginaceae family, Alexitoxicon darvasicum is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Boraginoideae.
uses: Alexitoxicon darvasicum is a plant native to the Darvas Mountains in Tajikistan, and its medicinal uses have not been extensively documented.
